behaviors.description: The conversation history passed to the LLM is trimmed to the most recent 10 messages when the full history exceeds the context window.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is deterministic trimming logic (selection/order/count), not real I/O. A unit test on the history-trimming function catches off-by-one, wrong-end slicing, and ordering bugs. Rejected higher level: behavior text is about 'trimmed to the most recent 10 messages' and does not require real DB/network/subprocess or real model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Call the trim function with histories of size 9, 10, 11, and larger; assert exactly the newest 10 are kept, in correct chronological order, and that boundary cases (exactly 10) are unchanged.

behaviors.description: The generated response addresses the customer's question rather than producing a generic or off-topic reply.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real LLM output relevance, which is non-deterministic and quality-based; stubs would always pass and hide real failures. Rejected Workflow: this behavior is a single generation quality check, not multiple agentic steps in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run prompts with representative customer questions against the real model, score relevance/off-topic rate with human rubric or calibrated evaluator, and fail if relevance falls below threshold across repeated runs.

behaviors.description: The tone re-check correctly identifies when a response is too formal for a casual-tier customer and triggers regeneration.
behaviors.justification: The 'correctly identifies' portion is model-judgment quality; mocked tone decisions can hide misclassification. Trigger wiring can be integration-tested, but minimum real-failure boundary here is the tone classifier/model behavior. Rejected Workflow: although regeneration exists, this behavior can be validated with one real tone-check decision plus assertion that regeneration is invoked.
behaviors.test_description: Provide a set of intentionally formal candidate replies for casual-tier users to the real tone-check path; verify high detection rate and that regeneration call is triggered when flagged.

behaviors.description: The full exchange (user message, generated response, any regenerations) is persisted to the database with correct timestamps.
behaviors.justification: Failure is at module handoff between chatbot orchestration and persistence schema/transaction behavior; a real local test DB is enough. Integration-brake checks: no required real OS/network/subprocess behavior beyond local DB; no AI quality dependency; no required multi-agentic chain for assertion.
behaviors.test_description: Execute message handling with one regeneration in a test environment using a real local DB; assert all records are stored (user input, initial draft, regenerated draft/final), linked to conversation, and timestamps are present/ordered correctly.

behaviors.description: When the tone check triggers regeneration, the second response replaces the first — the customer never sees the rejected draft.
behaviors.justification: Primary risk is orchestration/state handoff between tone-check result, regeneration, and response-delivery module. This can be caught with deterministic stubs for tone flag + model outputs; real model quality is not required for this behavior. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: behavior text is about replacement/visibility control ('never sees the rejected draft'), not evaluating model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Wire controller + tone-check decision + response renderer in-process; force tone-check flag true and two deterministic model outputs; assert outbound payload/UI/API contains only second response while first remains internal/auditable only.
